states.name: Idle
states.description: Initial state before CASE session establishment begins

states.name: Sigma1_Generated
states.description: Initiator has generated and sent Sigma1 message

states.name: Sigma1_Received
states.description: Responder has received and validated Sigma1 message

states.name: Sigma2_Generated
states.description: Responder has generated and sent Sigma2 message (full handshake)

states.name: Sigma2_Resume_Generated
states.description: Responder has generated and sent Sigma2_Resume message (resumption path)

states.name: Sigma2_Received
states.description: Initiator has received and validated Sigma2 message

states.name: Sigma2_Resume_Received
states.description: Initiator has received and validated Sigma2_Resume message

states.name: Sigma3_Generated
states.description: Initiator has generated and sent Sigma3 message

states.name: Sigma3_Received
states.description: Responder has received and validated Sigma3 message

states.name: Session_Keys_Established
states.description: Session encryption keys have been derived and are ready for use

states.name: Session_Active
states.description: CASE session successfully established and ready for application data

states.name: Validation_Failed
states.description: Protocol validation failed, session establishment aborted

states.name: Session_Evicted
states.description: Session evicted due to resource constraints (LRU policy)

states.name: BUSY_State
states.description: Responder is busy and cannot process new session request

transitions.guard_condition: has_noc == true && has_fabric_credentials == true && destination_node_known == true
actions: sigma1_msg := encode_sigma1(initiator_random, initiator_session_id, destination_id, initiator_eph_pubkey, resumption_id, initiator_resume_mic)
s2k_key := derive_s2k(ipk, msg2.responder_random, msg2.responder_eph_pubkey, sigma1_msg, shared_secret)
verify_resume2_mic(s2rk_key, resume_msg2.sigma2_resume_mic, initiator_random, resume_msg2.resumption_id, msg1, resume_msg2)

transitions.guard_condition: signature_verification_failed == true || certificate_validation_failed == true || aead_decrypt_failed == true
(msg1.resumption_id != null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic != null) || (msg1.resumption_id == null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic == null)
(msg1.resumption_id != null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic == null) || (msg1.resumption_id == null && msg1.initiator_resume_mic != null)

actions: s2k_key := derive_s2k(ipk, responder_random, responder_eph_keypair.public_key, msg1, shared_secret)

transitions.guard_condition: resumption_mode == true && resumption_validated == true && shared_secret != null
actions: sigma2_resume_mic := compute_resume_mic(s2rk_key, msg1.initiator_random, new_resumption_id, msg1, sigma2_resume_msg)

transitions.guard_condition: resumption_mode == true && (resumption_id_not_found == true || shared_secret_unavailable == true || resume_mic_verification_failed == true)
responder_noc_validated == true && certificate_chain_validated == true && signature_verified == true
aead_decrypt_failed == true || certificate_validation_failed == true || signature_verification_failed == true || fabric_id_mismatch == true

transitions.timing_requirement: SHALL evict LRU session based on SessionTimestamp

functions.name: generate_random

parameters.name: length_bytes
parameters.description: Number of random bytes to generate

functions.description: Generates cryptographically secure random bytes using Crypto_DRBG. Used for generating initiatorRandom, responderRandom, ResumptionID.
functions.algorithm_detail: DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator) per NIST SP 800-90A. Input: length in bits (length_bytes * 8). Output: random octet string of specified length. Assumes DRBG is properly seeded.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Generated transition for initiatorRandom, in Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated for responderRandom and ResumptionID

functions.name: allocate_unique_session_id
functions.description: Allocates a unique 16-bit session identifier that does not overlap with any existing PASE or CASE session identifiers in use by the node.
functions.algorithm_detail: Scans active session table, finds unused session ID in range [0x0001, 0xFFFF]. If all 65535 slots occupied, triggers session eviction using LRU policy. Session ID 0x0000 is reserved and SHALL NOT be used.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating Sigma1 (initiator) or Sigma2/Sigma2_Resume (responder) to create Local Session Identifier

functions.name: generate_ecdh_keypair
functions.description: Generates an ephemeral ECDH key pair on the P-256 elliptic curve for session key establishment.
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDH key generation on NIST P-256 curve (secp256r1). Generates private key (scalar) and computes public key (elliptic curve point). Public key encoded as uncompressed point (0x04 || x || y). Private key securely stored in memory.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Generated for initiatorEphKeyPair, in Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated for responderEphKeyPair

functions.name: compute_destination_identifier

parameters.name: root_public_key
parameters.description: Public key of trusted root CA certificate

functions.description: Computes Destination Identifier for privacy protection using IPK and destination information. Hides which Fabric was chosen by initiator while allowing responder to validate.
functions.algorithm_detail: destinationMessage = initiatorRandom || rootPublicKey || fabricId || nodeId (all little-endian). destination_identifier = Crypto_Hash(destinationMessage || IPK)[0:16]. Uses second-newest epoch key of IPK if available. Prevents linkability of CASE sessions to specific fabrics.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Generated transition to create Sigma1.destinationId field for privacy-preserving fabric selection

functions.name: lookup_resumption_id
functions.description: Searches for cached resumption state from previous CASE session with same peer on same fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Queries session resumption cache indexed by <fabric_id, peer_node_id>. Returns ResumptionID if valid cached session exists, null otherwise. Cache entry includes ResumptionID, SharedSecret, Local Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Peer CASE Authenticated Tags.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Generated to determine if session resumption is possible (optional optimization)

functions.name: compute_initiator_resume_mic

parameters.name: shared_secret
parameters.description: Shared secret from previous CASE session

functions.description: Computes Message Integrity Code for Sigma1 resumption request to prove possession of cached shared secret.
functions.algorithm_detail: S1RK = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=SharedSecret, salt=initiatorRandom||ResumptionID, info='Sigma1_Resume', len=128bits). Resume1MIC = Crypto_AEAD_GenerateEncrypt(K=S1RK, P=[], A=[], N='NCASE_SigmaS1')[MIC_only]. Note: Per spec hex bytes 0x53,0x31='S1', both generation and validation use NCASE_SigmaS1.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Generated when resumption_id exists to create Sigma1.initiatorResumeMIC field

functions.name: encode_sigma1
functions.description: Encodes Sigma1 message structure in TLV format with tag-order constraint.
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV encoding per Matter specification. Structure: {initiatorRandom[1], initiatorSessionId[2], destinationId[3], initiatorEphPubKey[4], initiatorSessionParams[5,optional], resumptionID[6,optional], initiatorResumeMIC[7,optional]}. Tag-order SHALL be preserved. Unknown context-specific tags SHALL be ignored.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Generated to create Sigma1 message for transmission

functions.name: send_message
functions.description: Sends protocol message over unsecured session using PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL with appropriate opcode and exchange flags.
functions.algorithm_detail: Messages sent with Session ID=0, Session Type=0 (unsecured). Initiator sets S Flag=1, DSIZ=0. Responder sets S Flag=0, DSIZ=1. Uses MRP for reliable messaging or TCP if both peers support it. Protocol ID=0x0000 (SECURE_CHANNEL). Opcode from Table 18 (Sigma1=0x30, Sigma2=0x31, Sigma3=0x32, Sigma2_Resume=0x33).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after encoding each Sigma message to transmit over network

functions.name: set_peer_session_identifier
functions.description: Sets Peer Session Identifier in Session Context for subsequent message routing.
functions.algorithm_detail: Stores peer's session identifier in session context. This value SHALL be used in Session ID field of every outgoing message on this session so peer can map to their Local Session Identifier.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving Sigma1 (responder), Sigma2 (initiator), or Sigma2_Resume (initiator) to record peer's session ID

functions.name: set_local_session_identifier
functions.description: Sets Local Session Identifier in Session Context for incoming message lookup.
functions.algorithm_detail: Stores local session identifier in session context. On incoming messages, Session ID field SHALL be used to map to this session context data for key recovery and authentication.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating session ID (Sigma1 for initiator, Sigma2/Sigma2_Resume for responder)

functions.name: compute_ecdh
functions.description: Computes ECDH shared secret from local private key and peer's public key.
functions.algorithm_detail: ECDH on NIST P-256 curve. SharedSecret = Crypto_ECDH(privateKey, publicKey). Output is x-coordinate of resulting elliptic curve point (32 bytes). Used as input key material for KDF operations. Ephemeral Diffie-Hellman provides forward secrecy.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Received (initiator) and Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated (responder) to establish shared secret

functions.name: derive_s2k

parameters.name: ipk
parameters.description: Identity Protection Key for the fabric

parameters.name: msg1
parameters.description: Complete Sigma1 message for transcript hash

functions.description: Derives S2K encryption key for protecting Sigma2 TBEData2 payload.
functions.algorithm_detail: TranscriptHash = Crypto_Hash(message = Msg1). S2K = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=SharedSecret, salt=IPK||responderRandom||responderEphPubKey||TranscriptHash, info='Sigma2', len=128bits). Uses HKDF-SHA256. S2K protects responder's NOC, ICAC, signature, and ResumptionID in Sigma2.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in both Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Received (decrypt) and Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated (encrypt)

functions.name: derive_s3k

parameters.name: ipk
parameters.description: Identity Protection Key for the fabric

functions.description: Derives S3K encryption key for protecting Sigma3 TBEData3 payload.
functions.algorithm_detail: TranscriptHash = Crypto_Hash(message = Msg1 || Msg2). S3K = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=SharedSecret, salt=IPK||TranscriptHash, info='Sigma3', len=128bits). Uses HKDF-SHA256. S3K protects initiator's NOC, ICAC, and signature in Sigma3.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma2_Received->Sigma3_Generated (encrypt) and Sigma2_Generated->Sigma3_Received (decrypt)

functions.name: derive_s2rk
functions.description: Derives S2RK resumption key for validating Sigma2_Resume message.
functions.algorithm_detail: S2RK = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=SharedSecret, salt=initiatorRandom||ResumptionID, info='Sigma2_Resume', len=128bits). Uses HKDF-SHA256. S2RK used to verify Resume2MIC proving responder knows cached SharedSecret without performing expensive signature operations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Resume_Received to validate Resume2MIC from responder

functions.name: encrypt_and_generate_aead
functions.description: Encrypts plaintext and generates authentication tag using AEAD cipher.
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_AEAD_GenerateEncrypt using AES-128-CCM. Inputs: K=key, P=plaintext, A=aad (empty for CASE), N=nonce. Output: ciphertext || MIC (16-byte tag). Nonce for Sigma2: 'NCASE_Sigma2N', Sigma3: 'NCASE_Sigma3N'. Provides authenticated encryption.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating Sigma2 or Sigma3 to encrypt TBEData structures

functions.name: decrypt_and_verify_aead
functions.description: Decrypts ciphertext and verifies authentication tag using AEAD cipher.
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_AEAD_DecryptVerify using AES-128-CCM. Inputs: K=key, C=ciphertext, A=aad (empty), N=nonce. Outputs: Success (boolean), Plaintext. Success=TRUE if MIC verification passes. Nonce must match encryption nonce. Constant-time MIC comparison prevents timing attacks.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating Sigma2 or Sigma3 to decrypt and authenticate TBEData structures

functions.name: validate_responder_noc
functions.description: Validates responder's NOC structure and constraints per Matter Certificate encoding rules.
functions.algorithm_detail: Verifies certificate DN encoding rules, checks Fabric ID and Node ID are within valid ranges. Validates certificate is properly formatted X.509v3 certificate with required extensions. Ensures PublicKey algorithm is ECDSA with P-256 curve. Checks validity period. Throws validation error if constraints violated.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Received when processing decrypted TBEData2

functions.name: validate_responder_icac
functions.description: Validates responder's ICAC structure and constraints if present.
functions.algorithm_detail: Verifies ICAC follows Matter Certificate encoding rules. Checks Basic Constraints extension indicates CA=TRUE. Validates Fabric ID in subject (if present) matches NOC Fabric ID. Ensures certificate chain is properly formed. Verifies validity period. Throws validation error if constraints violated.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Received if TBEData2 contains ICAC

functions.name: validate_certificate_chain

parameters.name: destination_or_fabric
parameters.description: Destination ID or Fabric context for validation

functions.description: Validates complete certificate chain from NOC to trusted root CA.
functions.algorithm_detail: Verifies certificate chain: NOC->ICAC(optional)->RCAC. Checks each certificate's signature using parent's public key. Validates Fabric ID and Node ID match destination identifier computation or matched fabric. Ensures RCAC public key matches key used in destination identifier. Verifies all certificates in chain respect Matter Certificate DN Encoding Rules including range checks. Throws validation error if chain invalid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma2_Received and Sigma3_Received after individual certificate validation

functions.name: verify_signature
functions.description: Verifies ECDSA signature over TBSData using peer's NOC public key.
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_Verify using ECDSA on P-256 curve with SHA-256 hash. Inputs: publicKey from NOC, message=TBSData, signature=ec-signature. Signature format: r||s (64 bytes, two 32-byte big-endian integers). Returns Success (boolean). Throws error if verification fails. Proves peer possesses NOC private key.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma2_Received (verify TBSData2Signature) and Sigma3_Received (verify TBSData3Signature)

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Creates ECDSA signature over TBSData using local NOC private key.
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_Sign using ECDSA on P-256 curve with SHA-256 hash. Inputs: message=TBSData, privateKey=NOKeyPair.privateKey. Output: signature as r||s (64 bytes). Deterministic nonce generation per RFC 6979 recommended. Proves possession of NOC private key to peer.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated (sign TBSData2) and Sigma2_Received->Sigma3_Generated (sign TBSData3)

functions.name: compute_hash
functions.description: Computes cryptographic hash of input data.
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_Hash using SHA-256. Input: arbitrary-length octet string. Output: 32-byte (256-bit) hash digest. Used for transcript hash computation: TranscriptHash = Crypto_Hash(Msg1 || Msg2 || Msg3) where || denotes concatenation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma3_Received->Session_Keys_Established and Sigma3_Generated->Session_Keys_Established to compute transcript hash for session key derivation

functions.name: derive_session_encryption_keys
functions.description: Derives final session encryption keys I2RKey, R2IKey, and AttestationChallenge.
functions.algorithm_detail: I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=SharedSecret, salt=IPK||TranscriptHash, info='SessionKeys', len=384bits). Uses HKDF-SHA256. Each output key is 128 bits. I2RKey encrypts initiator->responder messages. R2IKey encrypts responder->initiator messages. AttestationChallenge used for device attestation only.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma3_Received->Session_Keys_Established and Sigma3_Generated->Session_Keys_Established for full handshake

functions.name: derive_resumption_session_encryption_keys
functions.description: Derives session encryption keys for resumed session without full handshake.
functions.algorithm_detail: I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge = Crypto_KDF(inputKey=SharedSecret, salt=initiatorRandom||ResumptionID, info='SessionResumptionKeys', len=384bits). Uses HKDF-SHA256. Each output key is 128 bits. Different salt and info string from full handshake ensures key separation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma2_Resume_Received->Session_Keys_Established and Sigma2_Resume_Generated->Session_Keys_Established for session resumption path

functions.name: initialize_local_message_counter
functions.description: Initializes Local Message Counter in Session Context for outbound messages.
functions.algorithm_detail: Generates random 32-bit value using Crypto_DRBG. Sets Local Message Counter to this value. Message counter increments with each sent message, provides replay protection. Counter wraps at 2^32-1, requiring session re-establishment when exhausted.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma3_Received->Session_Keys_Established, Sigma3_Generated->Session_Keys_Established, and resumption paths before session becomes active

functions.name: initialize_message_reception_state
functions.description: Initializes Message Reception State in Session Context for tracking peer's message counter.
functions.algorithm_detail: Sets synchronized max_message_counter of peer to 0. Initializes window bitmap for out-of-order message detection. Provides replay protection for incoming messages. Peer's counter must be strictly increasing (modulo wrap).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma3_Received->Session_Keys_Established, Sigma2_Generated->Sigma3_Received, and Sigma2_Resume_Generated->Session_Keys_Established

functions.name: reset_message_reception_state
functions.description: Resets Message Reception State when resuming session.
functions.algorithm_detail: Resets synchronized max_message_counter to 0. Clears window bitmap. Required for session resumption to prevent replay attacks using messages from previous session instance.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma2_Resume_Received->Session_Keys_Established when initiator resumes session

functions.name: set_session_timestamp
functions.description: Sets SessionTimestamp to track when session was last active for LRU eviction.
functions.algorithm_detail: Records current timestamp from monotonic clock. SessionTimestamp updated whenever message sent or received on session. Used to determine least-recently-used session when resource eviction needed. Must allow comparison of relative session ages.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Session_Keys_Established transitions and updated throughout session lifetime

functions.name: get_operational_group_key
functions.description: Retrieves operational group key (IPK) for specified fabric and key preference.
functions.algorithm_detail: Looks up operational group key from Group Key Management Cluster KeySet with GroupKeySetID=0 (IPK). If second_newest_epoch_key requested and exists, returns key with second newest EpochStartTime. Otherwise returns single/newest key. IPK used exclusively for CASE Destination Identifier generation, not for group communication.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma3_Received->Session_Keys_Established and Sigma3_Generated->Session_Keys_Established to obtain IPK for session key derivation

functions.name: validate_destination_identifier
functions.description: Validates destination identifier matches one of responder's fabrics and returns matched fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: For each fabric responder belongs to: compute expected_destination_id = Crypto_Hash(initiatorRandom || rootPublicKey || fabricId || responderNodeId || IPK)[0:16] using second-newest epoch key if available. If destination_id matches any expected value, return that fabric reference. If no match found, send StatusReport(FAILURE, INVALID_PARAMETER) and abort.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Received when resumption mode is false to identify target fabric

functions.name: validate_resumption_parameters
functions.description: Validates resumption parameters and retrieves cached session state.
functions.algorithm_detail: Search for existing session with Resumption ID matching incoming resumptionID. If found, retrieve cached SharedSecret, Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, Peer CATs. Compute S1RK = Crypto_KDF(SharedSecret, initiatorRandom||ResumptionID, 'Sigma1_Resume', 128bits). Verify Resume1MIC via Crypto_AEAD_DecryptVerify(K=S1RK, C=Msg1.sigma1ResumeMIC, A=[], N='NCASE_SigmaS1'). If verification passes, return cached state. If fails or not found, fall back to full handshake.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->Sigma1_Received when both resumptionID and initiatorResumeMIC present in Sigma1

functions.name: compute_resume_mic
functions.description: Computes Resume2MIC for Sigma2_Resume message to prove responder knows SharedSecret.
functions.algorithm_detail: Resume2MIC = Crypto_AEAD_GenerateEncrypt(K=S2RK, P=[], A=[], N='NCASE_SigmaS2')[MIC_only]. Per spec, both P and A are empty byte arrays. Note: Per spec hex bytes 0x53,0x32='S2', both generation and validation use NCASE_SigmaS2.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Resume_Generated to create Sigma2_Resume.sigma2ResumeMIC

functions.name: verify_resume2_mic
functions.description: Verifies Resume2MIC from Sigma2_Resume message.
functions.algorithm_detail: Verify Resume2MIC via Crypto_AEAD_DecryptVerify(K=S2RK, C=ResumeMsg2.sigma2ResumeMIC, A=[], N='NCASE_SigmaS2'). If Success=FALSE, send StatusReport(FAILURE, INVALID_PARAMETER) and abort resumption.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Resume_Received to validate responder's Resume2MIC

functions.name: send_status_report

parameters.name: protocol_data
parameters.description: Optional protocol-specific data (e.g., retry time for BUSY)

functions.description: Sends StatusReport message to peer indicating protocol outcome or error.
functions.algorithm_detail: Encodes StatusReport with specified codes. For BUSY status, ProtocolData contains 16-bit little-endian retry time in milliseconds. For success, ProtocolCode=SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS. For failures, ProtocolCode=INVALID_PARAMETER. Sets appropriate Exchange ID and flags. Sent over unsecured session.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to send SigmaFinished (success), validation errors (INVALID_PARAMETER), or BUSY responses

functions.name: find_least_recently_used_session
functions.description: Finds session with oldest SessionTimestamp for eviction when resources exhausted.
functions.algorithm_detail: Scans all active sessions, compares SessionTimestamp values. Returns session with minimum (oldest) SessionTimestamp. This implements LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction policy. SessionTimestamp updated on every message send/receive, so truly represents last activity time.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Session_Active->Session_Evicted when new session requested and no free slots available

functions.name: cleanup_session_state

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: Session to cleanup (or current session if null)

functions.description: Cleans up session state, zeroes cryptographic keys, frees resources.
functions.algorithm_detail: Securely zeros all cryptographic material (I2RKey, R2IKey, SharedSecret, ephemeral keys). Removes session from active session table. Frees allocated memory. Invalidates session identifiers. Ensures no cryptographic material persists in memory after session termination.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions to Idle state, Session_Evicted, and error handling paths

functions.name: calculate_retry_delay
functions.description: Calculates appropriate retry delay in milliseconds for BUSY response.
functions.algorithm_detail: Determines minimum time before client should retry based on current load, pending requests, available resources. Returns 16-bit value representing milliseconds. Typical values range from 100ms to several seconds depending on resource pressure.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Idle->BUSY_State when responder cannot process new session request

functions.name: encode_sigma2
functions.description: Encodes Sigma2 message structure in TLV format.
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV encoding: {responderRandom[1], responderSessionId[2], responderEphPubKey[3], encrypted2[4], responderSessionParams[5,optional]}. Tag-order SHALL be preserved. Unknown context-specific tags SHALL be ignored.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated to create Sigma2 response

functions.name: encode_sigma2_resume
functions.description: Encodes Sigma2_Resume message structure in TLV format.
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV encoding: {resumptionID[1], sigma2ResumeMIC[2], responderSessionID[3], responderSessionParams[4,optional]}. Tag-order SHALL be preserved. Unknown context-specific tags SHALL be ignored.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Resume_Generated for session resumption path

functions.name: encode_sigma3
functions.description: Encodes Sigma3 message structure in TLV format.
functions.algorithm_detail: TLV encoding: {encrypted3[1]}. Simple structure containing only encrypted payload. Tag-order SHALL be preserved. Unknown context-specific tags SHALL be ignored.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Sigma2_Received->Sigma3_Generated to create Sigma3 message

functions.name: encode_tbsdata
functions.description: Encodes To-Be-Signed data structure for Sigma2 or Sigma3 signature.
functions.algorithm_detail: For Sigma2: {responderNOC[1], responderICAC[2,optional], responderEphPubKey[3], initiatorEphPubKey[4]}. For Sigma3: {initiatorNOC[1], initiatorICAC[2,optional], initiatorEphPubKey[3], responderEphPubKey[4]}. TLV encoded with tag-order. This structure is signed to prove possession of NOC private key.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating Sigma2 or Sigma3 signatures

functions.name: encode_tbedata
functions.description: Encodes To-Be-Encrypted data structure for Sigma2 or Sigma3 AEAD encryption.
functions.algorithm_detail: For Sigma2: {responderNOC[1], responderICAC[2,optional], signature[3], resumptionID[4]}. For Sigma3: {initiatorNOC[1], initiatorICAC[2,optional], signature[3]}. TLV encoded with tag-order. Certificate encodings SHALL be byte-for-byte identical to TBSData encodings. This structure is encrypted with AEAD.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when generating Sigma2 or Sigma3 encrypted payloads

definitions.definition: SIGMA (SIGn-and-MAc) is a cryptographic protocol pattern for authenticated key exchange. CASE uses SIGMA-inspired protocol with Identity Protection Key (IPK) for enhanced privacy. Provides mutual authentication, forward secrecy, and identity protection.
definitions.security_relevance: Foundation for CASE security properties. Ensures both peers authenticate each other's certificates while protecting fabric/node identities from passive observers.

definitions.definition: Operational group key under GroupKeySetID 0 used exclusively for CASE Destination Identifier generation. Provides privacy by masking which fabric initiator targets. Uses second-newest epoch key if available for rotation support.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for privacy protection. Prevents observers from linking CASE sessions to specific fabrics or tracking node communications across sessions.

definitions.definition: 16-byte value computed as Crypto_Hash(initiatorRandom || rootPublicKey || fabricId || nodeId || IPK)[0:16]. Allows responder to identify target fabric without revealing it to observers. Responder tries each fabric's expected value.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides fabric privacy. Only responder with matching fabric credentials can identify they are the intended target. Prevents fabric enumeration attacks.

definitions.definition: Optimization allowing re-establishment of session using cached SharedSecret from previous CASE session. Avoids expensive ECDH and signature operations. Uses ResumptionID and Resume MICs (Resume1MIC, Resume2MIC) for mutual authentication.
definitions.security_relevance: Reduces computational cost while maintaining security. Must properly validate cached state to prevent replay attacks. Falls back to full handshake if resumption fails.

definitions.definition: Output of ECDH key agreement between ephemeral key pairs. 32-byte value (x-coordinate of EC point). Used as input key material for all KDF operations. Saved for session resumption support. Provides forward secrecy.
definitions.security_relevance: Root secret for all session keys. Compromise of long-term NOC keys does not compromise past SharedSecrets due to ephemeral ECDH. Must be securely stored for resumption and securely erased when session ends.

definitions.definition: Structure containing NOC, optional ICAC, and both ephemeral public keys. Signed by NOC private key to prove possession. Different for Sigma2 (responder signs) and Sigma3 (initiator signs). Not transmitted, only signature sent.
definitions.security_relevance: Binds NOC to ephemeral keys, preventing man-in-the-middle attacks. Signature proves entity presenting NOC actually possesses corresponding private key.

definitions.definition: Structure containing NOC, optional ICAC, TBSData signature, and (for Sigma2) ResumptionID. Encrypted with AEAD using S2K (Sigma2) or S3K (Sigma3). Certificate encodings must be byte-for-byte identical to TBSData encodings.
definitions.security_relevance: Protects certificates and signatures during transmission. AEAD provides both confidentiality and authenticity. Prevents tampering with certificate chain.

definitions.definition: Symmetric encryption keys derived from SharedSecret for protecting TBEData2 and TBEData3. S2K uses salt=(IPK || responderRandom || responderEphPubKey || TranscriptHash) where TranscriptHash=Crypto_Hash(Msg1), info='Sigma2'. S3K uses salt=(IPK || TranscriptHash) where TranscriptHash=Crypto_Hash(Msg1||Msg2), info='Sigma3'. Both use HKDF-SHA256 with 128-bit output length.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides authenticated encryption for certificate exchange. Different keys and transcript hashes for Sigma2 and Sigma3 ensure cryptographic separation. Binding to IPK and transcript ensures key derivation is tied to specific fabric and handshake. Derived from ephemeral SharedSecret provides forward secrecy.

definitions.definition: Session encryption keys for application data. I2RKey encrypts initiator-to-responder messages. R2IKey encrypts responder-to-initiator messages. Derived from SharedSecret, IPK, and TranscriptHash. 128-bit keys for AES-CCM AEAD.
definitions.security_relevance: Protect all application data after session establishment. Binding to TranscriptHash prevents key reuse attacks. Directional keys provide cryptographic separation between communication directions.

definitions.definition: 16-byte random identifier for cached session state. Generated by responder in Sigma2, used by initiator in subsequent Sigma1 to request resumption. Index for looking up cached SharedSecret, Fabric Index, Peer Node ID, and CATs.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables session resumption without revealing session details to observers. Must be unpredictable to prevent session enumeration. Cached state must be validated with Resume MICs.

definitions.definition: Message Integrity Codes for resumption authentication. Resume1MIC computed by initiator using S1RK proves knowledge of cached SharedSecret. Resume2MIC computed by responder using S2RK provides mutual authentication. Both use AEAD with empty plaintext.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents resumption with stolen ResumptionID. Proves both parties possess cached SharedSecret without expensive signatures. Binds resumption to specific Sigma1 message via AAD.

definitions.definition: Resumption keys derived from cached SharedSecret. S1RK = KDF(SharedSecret, initiatorRandom||ResumptionID, 'Sigma1_Resume'). S2RK = KDF(SharedSecret, initiatorRandom||ResumptionID, 'Sigma2_Resume'). Used for Resume MIC computation.
definitions.security_relevance: Provide resumption authentication without full handshake. Different keys for each direction ensure cryptographic separation. Binding to initiatorRandom prevents replay attacks across resumption attempts.

definitions.definition: SHA-256 hash of concatenated Sigma1, Sigma2, and Sigma3 messages. Computed as Crypto_Hash(Msg1 || Msg2 || Msg3). Used as salt input to session key derivation. Binds session keys to complete handshake transcript.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents tampering with handshake messages. Ensures session keys depend on all exchanged messages. Provides implicit authentication of entire handshake sequence.

definitions.definition: 16-byte value derived alongside I2RKey and R2IKey. Used exclusively for Device Attestation Procedure during commissioning. Not used for message encryption/decryption. Third output of session key KDF.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides fresh challenge for device attestation. Binding to session establishment prevents replay of attestation data across sessions. Must only be used for attestation, not encryption.

definitions.definition: 32-bit counter initialized randomly at session establishment. Increments with each sent message. Part of AEAD nonce: Security Flags (1 byte) || Message Counter (4 bytes) || Source Node ID (8 bytes). Provides replay protection.
definitions.security_relevance: Critical for preventing replay attacks. Combined with Source Node ID in nonce ensures nonce uniqueness. Session must be re-established when counter exhausts (wraps at 2^32).

definitions.definition: Least Recently Used eviction policy for session management. When resources exhausted and new session requested, evicts session with oldest SessionTimestamp. SessionTimestamp updated on every message send/receive.
definitions.security_relevance: Ensures fair resource allocation. Prevents denial-of-service by blocking new sessions. Maintains most actively used sessions. Evicted session notified via StatusReport.

definitions.definition: StatusReport with GeneralCode=BUSY sent when responder cannot process new session request. Includes ProtocolData with 16-bit little-endian retry delay in milliseconds. Initiator should wait specified time before retrying.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents resource exhaustion attacks. Allows graceful handling of temporary overload. Retry time prevents thundering herd problem when multiple initiators retry simultaneously.

definitions.definition: X.509v3 certificate containing Node's operational identity on a Fabric. Includes Fabric ID and Node ID in subject DN. Public key used for ECDSA signature verification. Private key proves identity during CASE.
definitions.security_relevance: Foundation of node authentication. Binds node to specific Fabric. Used to verify TBSData signatures. Must be protected against unauthorized access or tampering.

definitions.definition: Optional X.509v3 certificate in chain between NOC and RCAC. Basic Constraints must indicate CA=TRUE. May contain Fabric ID in subject. Enables hierarchical certificate structure.
definitions.security_relevance: Extends trust chain for larger deployments. Must be validated to prevent unauthorized certificate issuance. Fabric ID (if present) must match NOC Fabric ID.

definitions.definition: Trusted Root Certificate Authority certificate. Self-signed X.509v3 certificate at top of trust chain. Public key used in Destination Identifier computation and certificate chain validation.
definitions.security_relevance: Root of trust for entire Fabric. Compromise of RCAC private key compromises all nodes in Fabric. Must be pre-installed or provisioned securely during commissioning.

security_properties.description: Both initiator and responder mutually authenticate each other's operational certificates and prove possession of corresponding private keys through ECDSA signatures over TBSData.
Ephemeral ECDH key exchange ensures compromise of long-term NOC private keys does not compromise past session keys. Each session uses freshly generated ephemeral keys.
Destination Identifier hides target fabric from passive observers using IPK. Only responder with matching fabric credentials can identify they are intended target.

Complete certificate chain from NOC through optional ICAC to trusted RCAC must be validated. All certificates must satisfy Matter Certificate DN Encoding Rules including Fabric ID and Node ID range checks.
Message counters with window-based replay detection prevent replay of encrypted messages. Counters initialized randomly and must be strictly increasing.
Session resumption maintains security while avoiding expensive operations. Resume MICs prove both parties possess cached SharedSecret without full handshake.
Session keys derived from TranscriptHash binding ensures keys depend on complete handshake transcript. Prevents tampering with any handshake message.
TBEData structures protected with AEAD (AES-CCM) providing both confidentiality and authenticity. AEAD verification failure causes session abort.
When session resources exhausted, least recently used session evicted based on SessionTimestamp. Ensures fairness and prevents resource starvation.
critical_transitions: Session_Active->Session_Evicted (evict LRU session when resources exhausted)

security_properties.description: All validation failures (signature verification, certificate validation, AEAD decryption) result in StatusReport with INVALID_PARAMETER and session abort. No partial state preserved.
Responder can signal resource exhaustion via BUSY StatusReport with retry delay. Prevents resource exhaustion attacks while allowing graceful degradation.
critical_transitions: Idle->BUSY_State (responder too busy for new session)

security_properties.description: Session identifiers must be unique across all active PASE and CASE sessions on a node. Session ID space shared between PASE and CASE.
For Destination Identifier generation, use operational group key with second newest EpochStartTime if exists, otherwise use single/newest key. Supports key rotation.

AEAD nonces must be unique per key usage. For session messages: Security Flags || Message Counter || Source Node ID. For handshake: constant nonces ('NCASE_Sigma2N', 'NCASE_Sigma3N') with ephemeral keys.
Different KDF info strings for S2K, S3K, S1RK, S2RK, session keys, resumption keys ensure cryptographic separation. Keys cannot be confused or reused across contexts.
critical_transitions: All key derivation transitions use unique info strings

security_properties.description: If session resumption fails (ResumptionID not found, SharedSecret unavailable, Resume MIC verification failed), responder falls back to full handshake automatically.
critical_transitions: Sigma1_Received->Sigma2_Generated (fallback to full handshake when resumption impossible)

security_properties.description: Fabric ID and Node ID in certificates must match destination identifier computation or previously matched fabric. Prevents cross-fabric attacks.
critical_transitions: Sigma1_Generated->Sigma2_Received (verify Fabric ID/Node ID match destination)
Sigma2_Generated->Sigma3_Received (verify Fabric ID matches earlier determination)

security_properties.description: All CASE message structures encoded with tag-order constraint. Unknown context-specific tags silently ignored for forward compatibility.
critical_transitions: All message encoding transitions must preserve tag order

security_properties.description: SessionTimestamp updated on every message send/receive. Used for LRU eviction and session age determination. Must use clock allowing relative comparison.
All cryptographic material (ephemeral keys, SharedSecret, session keys) securely zeroed when session ends or validation fails. Prevents key leakage from memory.

cryptographic_operations.output: shared_secret (32 bytes, x-coordinate of EC point)
assumptions: Ephemeral keys freshly generated per session

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA on NIST P-256 curve with SHA-256 hash
cryptographic_operations.output: signature (64 bytes: r||s, two 32-byte big-endian integers)
assumptions: ECDSA is unforgeable under chosen message attack

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: ECDSA on NIST P-256 curve with SHA-256 hash
inputs: public_key from NOC (65 bytes uncompressed point)

assumptions: Info strings are unique per context
Nonce never reused with same key

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Deterministic Random Bit Generator per NIST SP 800-90A

assumptions: Second-newest epoch key used if available

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: AES-128-CCM AEAD with empty plaintext (MAC-only mode)
inputs: nonce (constant 'NCASE_SigmaS1' for Resume1MIC or 'NCASE_SigmaS2' for Resume2MIC)
assumptions: AEAD in MAC-only mode provides secure authentication
Nonce constants prevent confusion between Resume1MIC and Resume2MIC

security_assumptions.assumption: Cryptographic primitives (ECDH, ECDSA, HKDF, AES-CCM, SHA-256) are secure
security_assumptions.if_violated: Complete protocol security breakdown. Attacker could forge signatures, break encryption, or compromise key derivation.

security_assumptions.assumption: NOC private keys are securely stored and never leaked
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker with NOC private key can impersonate node, decrypt past sessions if SharedSecret stored, and sign fraudulent certificates.

security_assumptions.assumption: Trusted RCAC certificates are authentic and properly provisioned
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker with rogue RCAC can issue fraudulent NOCs, impersonate any node in fabric, and intercept communications.

security_assumptions.assumption: DRBG is properly seeded with sufficient entropy
security_assumptions.if_violated: Predictable random values enable replay attacks, session hijacking, and compromise of ephemeral keys.

security_assumptions.assumption: System clock provides monotonic timestamps for SessionTimestamp
security_assumptions.if_violated: LRU eviction may not work correctly. Attacker manipulating clock could prevent eviction or force eviction of active sessions.

security_assumptions.assumption: Message counters do not wrap during single session lifetime
security_assumptions.if_violated: Counter wrap causes nonce reuse with same key, breaking AEAD security. Session must be re-established before counter exhausts.

security_assumptions.assumption: IPK (operational group key) is kept confidential within fabric
security_assumptions.if_violated: Destination Identifier privacy lost. Attacker can link sessions to specific fabrics and track node communications.

security_assumptions.assumption: Certificate validation correctly checks all constraints (DN encoding, Fabric ID/Node ID ranges, validity periods)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malformed or expired certificates could be accepted. Attacker could use certificates outside intended scope or bypass access controls.

security_assumptions.assumption: Memory is securely erased when session ends
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cryptographic keys remain in memory. Attacker with memory access (cold boot attack, debugging) could recover session keys or SharedSecret.

security_assumptions.assumption: Implementation uses constant-time comparison for MIC and signature verification
security_assumptions.if_violated: Timing side channels could leak information about keys or MACs. Attacker could mount timing attacks to forge signatures or bypass authentication.

notes: CASE protocol uses SIGMA-inspired authenticated key exchange with Identity Protection Key for privacy
Session resumption is optional optimization avoiding expensive signature operations
All TLV structures must preserve tag-order; unknown context-specific tags silently ignored
Certificate chain validation critical for security: NOC->ICAC(optional)->RCAC
Ephemeral ECDH provides forward secrecy; long-term NOC compromise does not affect past sessions
Message counters initialized randomly and must not wrap during session lifetime
LRU eviction based on SessionTimestamp ensures fairness when resources exhausted
BUSY status with retry delay prevents resource exhaustion attacks
All validation failures result in StatusReport and session abort
Session keys bound to transcript hash prevent tampering with handshake messages
IPK uses second-newest epoch key if available to support key rotation
AEAD nonces must be unique: constant for handshake (with ephemeral keys), counter-based for session messages
Different KDF info strings ensure cryptographic separation across contexts
Secure key erasure required when session ends to prevent memory-based attacks
